title: change podman to implement macOS new container API
body:
### Feature request description

Apple announced a couple days ago at WWDC that they wrote a new container API/utility https://github.com/apple/container, are there plans to consider utilizing this within podman to possibly improve podman on macOS?

### Suggest potential solution

Look at changing how podman works currently on macOS and utilize what Apple recently released to improve podman on mac.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

the alternative would be to ignore it and make no changes

### Additional context

none

comments.body: There are no plans for us to embrace that on Macs at this time.  There are functionally still quite a ways behind what people would expect from their container runtimes.

The fellow CNCF Project [Lima](https://lima-vm.io/) came to the same conclusion:

* https://github.com/lima-vm/lima/discussions/3622

It could be an interesting alternative in the future, like WSL2?

It's running container **images**, not [containers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OS-level_virtualization).

You can run podman containers inside apple "containers".

Like if you really want to, as an alternative to `podman machine`.

```Dockerfile
FROM fedora
RUN dnf install -y podman
```

Advanced features might need custom kernels, like with bootc.
